Commercial Apr 12, 2026

Report: Southwest's Cheapest Fares May Soon Come With No Overhead Bin Access

The move would eliminate free carry-ons for Basic fares.

Southwest's potential overhead bin restrictions signal an industry shift toward unbundling amenities historically included in base fares to compete with ultra-low-cost carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

Do fast Wi-Fi and the shift in media consumption portend BYOL IFE?

If a major streaming platform ever owned a major Hollywood studio, would it change the trajectory of inflight streaming and by extension, the trajectory of the inflight entertainment and connectivity market? With the world’s top two streaming platforms, Netflix and Amazon Prime, firmly in mind, and...

Airlines' IFE strategies may fundamentally shift if streaming giants acquire content studios, reducing dependency on traditional airline-curated entertainment offerings.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

The Airline With The Top Short-Haul Widebody Business Classes In 2026

The oneworld carrier's new regional business class concept sets it apart.

Airlines are reimagining regional widebody cabins as premium revenue opportunities, recognizing short-haul business travel's willingness to pay for enhanced comfort despite shorter flight times.

See Simple Flying →

International Apr 12, 2026

Gulf Air and Iraqi Airways resumes operations

Days after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire in the Middle East, airspaces across the region have opened, allowing carriers such as Gulf Air and Iraqi Airways to resume limited scheduled operations from its home bases. Gulf Air Resumes Home Base Ops via Riyadh Earlier in March 2026, the...

Regional carriers are capitalizing on conflict de-escalation to rebuild networks, signaling gradual Middle East aviation market recovery dependent on geopolitical stability.

See Travel Radar →

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

Lufthansa pilots plan two-day strike as tensions rise

Pilots at Lufthansa announced a two-day strike in Germany, marking a further escalation in an already tense labour…...

Lufthansa's pilot strike signals escalating labor disputes that could disrupt European air travel and increase operational costs across the network.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Apr 12, 2026

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Understanding Part 5's structured approach to safety risk assessment enables operators to systematically identify and mitigate hazards before incidents occur.

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

Adieu, A330: Air France Sets Retirement Date After Quarter Century Of Service

The few remaining A330s will be retired by next summer, and their flights are set to be replaced by Airbus A350-900s and Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners.

Air France's A330 retirement accelerates widebody fleet modernization, reducing maintenance complexity while transitioning to next-gen fuel-efficient aircraft with 20-25% better economics.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Apr 12, 2026

Why One Of The World's Largest Freighter Operators Just Broke Up With Boeing For Airbus

This sudden shift in allegiance could signal a changing tide in the freighter market.

FedEx or UPS's switch to Airbus freighters suggests Boeing's production delays and 737 MAX cargo variant issues are now forcing major operators to diversify suppliers despite decades of Boeing relationships.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Apr 12, 2026

UK marks 90 years since historic flight of Spitfire aircraft

A commemorative Spitfire flight arrived at Glasgow Prestwick Airport (PIK) as part of a nationwide 90th anniversary on April 10, 2026. The spitfire is a notable piece of British aviation history. Marking 90 years of Spitfire Flight The two-seat Spitfire, painted in the colours of the original...

The Spitfire's 90-year legacy demonstrates the enduring cultural significance of heritage aircraft in maintaining public engagement with aviation history across UK regions.

See Travel Radar →

PAI
Safety Apr 12, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must formally document why selected risk controls are adequate rather than implementing every theoretically possible mitigation, balancing safety effectiveness against operational feasibility.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Safety Apr 12, 2026

USAF C-130 Hercules Sustains 'Extensive Damage' In Hatchet Attack At Shannon Airport

A man armed with a hatchet infiltrated Shannon Airport, climbing onto a parked USAF C-130 Hercules.

Airport perimeter security gaps allowed ground-level access to a military transport aircraft, exposing critical vulnerabilities in airfield infrastructure protection protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

United Airlines' Longest Nonstop Routes With The Boeing 787 Dreamliner In 2026

An aircraft that has proven its capability across all corners of the globe for a powerhouse of aviation. Here is where United operates the Dreamliner.

United's 787 expansion in 2026 signals growing confidence in ultra-long-haul profitability as carriers pursue premium transatlantic and transpacific markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 12, 2026

Why The Airbus A350 Is Becoming The Go-To Aircraft For Ultra-Long-Haul Routes

The aircraft offers exceptional ultra-long-range capabilities.

The A350's fuel efficiency and cabin design enable airlines to profitably operate routes previously requiring stopovers, reshaping long-haul network planning.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Apr 12, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must balance operational efficiency against dense residential areas and complex Bay Area airspace constraints.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

Alaska Airlines To Build New $135 Million Maintenance Hangar In Portland

The new maintenance hangar will not only benefit Alaska Airlines but also the local community.

Alaska Airlines' Portland investment signals confidence in sustained regional demand and establishes a major maintenance hub to reduce aircraft downtime across its Pacific Northwest operations.

See Simple Flying →

International Apr 11, 2026

Dubai Limits Flights Until May 31 Disrupting Indian Airlines

Dubai has introduced new limits on international flights, restricting foreign carriers to just one daily service to its airports until May 31, as regional tensions continue to impact aviation. This move is expected to significantly affect airline schedules and passenger travel across one of the...

Regional geopolitical instability is forcing major aviation hubs to implement supply-side constraints that shift competitive dynamics toward carriers with established slot priorities.

See Travel Radar →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

Why Avelo Airlines Scrapped All International Flights

The carrier has reverted to a domestic focus.

Avelo's retreat from international operations signals the financial pressures facing ultra-low-cost carriers in competing on longer routes with higher infrastructure demands.

See Simple Flying →

▶
General Aviation Apr 12, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Medium-hub airports like Cedar Rapids serve as critical infrastructure for both flight training pipelines and commercial air service, making them essential economic engines for regional aviation ecosystems.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

The New Baggage Policy Changes That Delta Air Lines Passengers Should Know About In 2026

Everyone from first-timers to frequent flyers should be aware of these alterations.

Delta's 2026 baggage policy shifts will likely reshape ancillary revenue models across the industry, prompting competitors to recalibrate their own baggage fee strategies.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Apr 11, 2026

Palma Airport to Introduce Separate Lanes for British Citizens

Passengers travelling from the UK through Palma de Mallorca Airport (PMI) are set to see new dedicated passport lanes. This move shows Spain preparing for major changes to border control systems across the European Union. Dedicated Lanes to Manage Passenger Flow The airport confirmed it will...

Spanish airports are proactively implementing dedicated immigration infrastructure ahead of EU-wide border control digitalization, which will require airlines and ground handlers to adapt operational planning.

See Travel Radar →

Military Apr 11, 2026

Exclusive: Interview with the Chief Test Pilot of VMX-1

On the occasion of the MCAS Yuma airshow 2026, we had the opportunity to interview the Chief Operational Test Director and Chief Test Pilot of Marine Operational Test & Evaluation Squadron (VMX) 1. After speaking last year with the Commanding Officer of VMX-1, Colonel John “Ike” Dirk, we realized...

VMX-1's test pilots provide critical operational validation for Marine Corps aircraft before fleet deployment, directly shaping tactical readiness and combat effectiveness.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Apr 12, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers—not just aircraft design—represents the most immediately actionable path to meeting sustainability demands amid projected traffic growth.

See MDPI →

International Apr 11, 2026

Dublin Airport warns passengers about protest delays on M50

On April 10, Dublin Airport (DUB) issued warnings urging passengers to allow extra time for their journey as the current national fuel protest will cause delays. The commuting routes into Dublin will be affected and other major roads around the country. Passengers warned of major delays in...

Airport operators must now factor protest-induced road congestion into passenger advisories, as ground transportation disruptions increasingly impact on-time arrival rates independent of airside operations.

See Travel Radar →

Aerospace Apr 11, 2026

Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS

A Falcon 9 launched a Cygnus cargo spacecraft April 11 as Northrop Grumman continues its dependence on a competitor to fly resupply missions to the International Space Station.

Northrop Grumman's reliance on SpaceX launch services highlights the competitive consolidation in commercial space logistics despite developing its own cargo vehicle.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

How Ryanair Enforces Cabin Baggage Rules

Air travel in Europe has become synonymous with low fares, but also with increasingly strict baggage rules. Ryanair, in particular, has turned enforcement into a highly visible part of the flying experience. According to the BBC and The Guardian, ground staff earn €2.50 for every piece of oversized...

Ryanair's commission-based enforcement model financially incentivizes ground staff to reject oversized bags, creating potential revenue conflicts between baggage compliance and customer service standards.

See Travel Radar →

Aerospace Apr 11, 2026

La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords

Los primeros astronautas en viajar a la Luna en más de medio siglo han regresado a la Tierra tras una misión que ha establecido nuevos récords a bordo del vuelo de prueba Artemis II de la NASA. Los astronautas de la NASA Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover y Christina Koch, y el astronauta Jeremy Hansen de...

Artemis II's successful return validates NASA's deep-space exploration architecture, demonstrating human-rated systems for lunar missions and establishing operational baselines for sustained crewed lunar operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 11, 2026

NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24

NASA is sending more science, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies to the International Space Station following the successful launch of the agency’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24. Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying...

Northrop Grumman's expanded Cygnus XL variant demonstrates commercial cargo providers' increasing payload capacity to support NASA's ISS operations and research expansion.

See NASA →

Industry Apr 11, 2026

Fast-growing RAVE Aerospace identifies industry choke points

Enjoying robust demand for its RAVE series of embedded inflight entertainment systems, RAVE Aerospace has identified widespread memory chip shortages and safety regulators’ more stringent head injury criterion (HIC) certification requirements as among the challenges presently faced by aircraft...

Supply chain bottlenecks in memory chips and stricter HIC safety standards are emerging as critical barriers limiting growth for IFE system manufacturers despite strong market demand.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

Lufthansa Strike Disrupts Flights at Frankfurt and Munich Hubs

Lufthansa is facing new disruptions as cabin crew walk out for the third time in two months, grounding flights across its main German hubs, Frankfurt and Munich. The strike on Friday, April 10, 2026, affected services scheduled between 12:00 and 22:00 (CEST), with widespread cancellations expected....

Repeated labor actions targeting Lufthansa's hub operations signal escalating wage disputes that could reshape European airline labor dynamics and operational planning.

See Travel Radar →

Aerospace Apr 11, 2026

Artemis II Splashes Down

This image from April 10, 2026, captures NASA’s Orion spacecraft, with its parachutes deployed, seconds before splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. The Artemis II crew accomplished many milestones on their nearly 10-day mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 record for farthest crewed spaceflight and...

Artemis II's successful Pacific splashdown validates NASA's deep-space human spaceflight architecture for lunar return missions beyond Apollo-era capabilities.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

American Airlines Join US Airlines Raising Fees

U.S. airlines are passing rising fuel costs on to travellers through higher baggage fees, with American Airlines and Alaska Air among the latest carriers to raise prices. This latest moves come as jet fuel prices have surged sharply since February, driven by conflict in the Middle East and wider...

Rising geopolitical tensions are triggering a cascade of ancillary revenue increases across major carriers, signaling that fuel surcharges may become structural rather than temporary.

See Travel Radar →

Military Apr 11, 2026

New Images Provide Clearest View Yet of Damage to U.S.-made AN/FPS-132 Early Warning Radar in Qatar

The imagery offers a closer look at the damage to the giant phased-array AN/FPS-132 radar struck during Iran’s attack on Qatar last month. Newly published photos by Al Jazeera circulated online offer the clearest view yet t0 the damage sustained by the U.S.-made AN/FPS-132 Block 5 (SSPARS)...

The AN/FPS-132 radar's vulnerability to direct strikes reveals critical gaps in air defense infrastructure protection strategies for allied Middle Eastern air operations.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

JetSMART Introduces “All You Can Fly” Subscription Model

JetSMART is reshaping low-cost travel in South America with the launch of its new “All You Can Fly” membership. The airline announced the plan on April 9, offering passengers unlimited flights for a fixed annual fee. A New Way to Travel More for Less This plan allows travellers to pay once and...

JetSMART's unlimited subscription model addresses South America's price-sensitive market by converting unpredictable airfare revenue into predictable recurring memberships, potentially pressuring competitors to adopt similar loyalty mechanisms.

See Travel Radar →

Commercial Apr 11, 2026

Separate Operations in Oman Air’s Acquisition of SalamAir

SalamAir has been acquired by Oman’s government as part of an integration process. According to the government, the acquisition aims to form a “sustainable national aviation sector,” though Oman Air and SalamAir will remain independent in both operations and branding for the time being. The origins...

Oman's strategic acquisition maintains SalamAir's operational independence rather than merging it into Oman Air, suggesting a portfolio approach to capturing both premium and budget-conscious market segments.

See Travel Radar →

General Aviation Apr 11, 2026

AOPA presents draft governance changes to members

AOPA presented a set of bylaw changes to members, seeking feedback on a set of significant potential revisions, including changes to the process for nominating members to the Board of Trustees, setting term limits, and establishing required trustee qualifications to serve on the Board.

AOPA's proposed governance reforms signal an effort to professionalize board selection and prevent entrenchment, which could reshape how general aviation's largest advocacy organization allocates resources and sets priorities.

See AOPA News →

Aerospace Apr 11, 2026

NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth

The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen splashed down at...

Artemis II's successful crewed lunar return validates NASA's deep space exploration architecture, directly enabling near-term commercial lunar logistics and resource utilization opportunities.

See NASA →

The move would eliminate free carry-ons for Basic fares.

NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth

The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen splashed down at...

Artemis II's successful crewed lunar return validates NASA's deep space exploration architecture, directly enabling near-term commercial lunar logistics and resource utilization opportunities.

See NASA →
